sections.paragraph:
πού enclitic adv. (Hom.+; pap, LXX; TestSol 1, 46; Just.; Tat. 37, 1; Mel., P. 72, 530.)

① marker of an undetermined position or place, somewhere w. quotations (Diod S 1, 12, 10 ‘the poet’ [=Homer] says ποὺ κατὰ τὴν ποίησιν=somewhere in his poem. Of Cercidas [III b.c.] [ed. Diehl3 Fgm. 11a, 4] ὀρθῶς λέγει που Κερκίδας [quot. follows]. Lucian, Ver. Hist. 2, 42 φησὶ γάρ που κἀκεῖνος [i.e. Antimachus IV b.c.], then a quot.; Appian, Bell. Civ. 1, 97 §452 [with a quot.]; Plut., Mor. 553b [Homeric quot.]; Philo, Ebr. 61 εἶπε γάρ πού τις, and Gen 20:12 follows; Just. A I, 3, 3 ἔφη γάρ που καί τις) Hb 2:6; 4:4; 1 Cl 15:2; 21:2; 26:2; 28:2; 42:5.—After a neg.=nowhere Dg 5:2. W. weakening of the local mng. εἰ δέ που παρηκολουθώς τις τοῖς πρεσβυτέροις ἔλθοι when perchance someone came who had been associated with the ‘elders’ Papias (2:4).

② marker of numerical approximation, about, approximately (Paus. 8, 11, 14 περὶ εἴκοσί που σταδίους; Aelian, VH 13, 4; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 104) Ro 4:19.—On δή π., μή π. s. δήπου, μήπου.—μή που (some edd. μήπου; v.l. μήπως. Hom. et al.; BGU 446, 15; Jos., Bell. 7, 397, Ant. 18, 183) conj. lest φοβεῖσθαι μ. π. Ac 27:29.—DELG s.v. πο-. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Cercidas Cercidas , III b.c.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

quot. quot. = quotation

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

neg. neg. = negative

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

edd. edd. = editions, editors

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

conj. conj. = conjunction

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 858
